authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA FAQ is the current authoritative public explanation of who must keep traceability plans and records, record-retrieval expectations, and how covered entities remain responsible even when another party maintains records on their behalf.
used_for: authoritative ingredient lot and shipment evidence
handoff prerequisites for covered foods and holders

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for records, holding, distribution, preventive controls, and supply-chain-program boundaries in food manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality hold, release, records, and supplier-program boundary review
used_for: where coordination stops and formal quality or supply-chain-program ownership begins

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA guidance is authoritative operating guidance for preventive-controls implementation and supply-chain-program handling, including supplier-verification expectations that constrain this lane.
used_for: formal quality or supplier-program ownership triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when affected ingredient inventory or downstream shipments require stop-and-escalate handling rather than continued routing.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise traceability and identifier exchanges used widely across food and beverage ingredient supply networks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system lot and event evidence comparison

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public event-vocabulary reference for consistent business-step and disposition semantics across ERP, WMS, MES, and partner handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code - Food Manufacturing, Edition 10
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQF is a recognized operating reference used broadly in food manufacturing to define supplier controls, change handling, audit evidence, and nonconformance expectations beyond bare regulatory minimums.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, prior owner, next owner candidate, required records, ingredient scope, supplier context, and tenant authority rules before any handoff or closure claim.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize system evidence and terminology, confirm owner transitions, and prepare handoff or status outputs with explicit ingredient, supplier, and logistics artifact requirements.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware blocker and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve ingredient lot, inventory, supplier, COA, allergen, and quality evidence when conflicts, holds, nonconformance, or substitution pressure appear so the lane escalates instead of guessing.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, required records, and blocker resolution are explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, formula or BOM demand, and schedule commitments
ingredient item masters, approved-supplier lists, and supplier records
inventory states, warehouse allocations, inbound-shipment records, and traceability logs
COAs, allergen statements, and ingredient specifications
quality holds, release records, and nonconformance reports
handoff summaries, blocker logs, status updates, and destination-owner confirmations

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for ingredient supply coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, and blocker states for ingredient handoffs
summarize traceability, inventory, supplier, inbound-logistics, and release dependencies without implying final execution or approval authority
escalate: quality hold, recall, release, allergen, or nonconformance decisions requiring formal quality authority
supplier approval, ingredient substitution approval, or formulation or engineering change authority
conflicting public and tenant policy that materially changes hold, release, quarantine, or ownership handling
refuse: bypass traceability, release, allergen, or quality controls to preserve throughput or service levels
approve held or unreleased ingredient for use, movement, or substitution
treat supplier nonconformance or substitute-material approval as coordination-only when formal approval is required

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative ingredient lot, approved-supplier, COA, release, or destination-owner evidence.
Conflicting ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of a blocker.
A quality hold, missing COA, or release boundary is treated as a scheduling inconvenience rather than a formal stop condition.
Supplier substitution, allergen-sensitive replacement, or supplier nonconformance is handled as routine coordination instead of routed to supplier-quality or formulation owners.
The lane absorbs procurement, logistics, planning, or quality execution work instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right destination owner accepted the required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Plant-specific release and quarantine movement rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Sites differ on whether held ingredient lots may be moved, relabeled, sampled, or staged before formal disposition, which changes routing advice.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant hold and release maps at runtime and block confident routing when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Approved substitute-material rules and allergen constraints
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant formula governance, allergen controls, and substitute-material policies can change whether a supply continuity request is routine coordination or a formal change-control event.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant substitution, allergen, and formulation-control rules before any substitution-oriented routing is treated as safe.

notes: Authoritative records for this lane are the tenant-designated ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, and logistics records constrained by FDA, eCFR, GS1, and SQF public baselines.
The lane stops before final quality release, recall decisions, supplier approval, formulation approval, or direct procurement and logistics execution even under shortage pressure.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because each open point converts into explicit tenant-required facts and orchestrator-return rules.
